programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Miami detective Horatio Caine enlists Mac's help as he pursues serial killer Henry Darius in New York. However, he soon loses the trail and is forced to follow other leads. An investigation into the sister of one of Darius's victims proves a dead end, but the team soon finds a vital link between the murderer and one of his hostages. David Caruso guest stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Jenny Shepard tries to solve the murder of an underworld source who was prepared to give information on the activities of arms dealer La Grenouille. Unfortunately her behaviour becomes increasingly obsessive as the case proceeds, leaving Gibbs wondering if her judgement is being clouded by a personal motivation. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's friend is the main suspect when an archaeologist is murdered. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen is put in touch with a former KGB operative who reveals a billionaire philanthropist is set to receive a shipment of counterfeit computer chips that could have disastrous consequences for the US military

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and the team try to prove Ziva had nothing to do with a political assassination carried out by her former Mossad partner - but have to tackle the case without Gibbs, who has retired to Mexico and now spends his time building boats. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a Navy reservist returns home to find her husband missing and her living room covered in blood, and make a grim discovery when they find a finger in the bin. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tracks Bodnar's SUV to a remote house in the countryside, but inside find only his dead accomplice - identified as a South African mercenary who helped Bodnar procure diamonds. Ziva and Tony, meanwhile, discharge themselves from hospital and wheedle information out of Abby to continue investigating on the quiet, having been warned by Homeland Security to stay off the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

programme.desc: Following an explosion at a cinema, Avery and her team go in search of a bomber who intends to expose the truth about society's over-reliance on technology. Patricia Arquette and James Van Der Beek star

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: The Bill

Janet is determined to secure her release, but Dr Cameron doubts the wisdom of her efforts. Drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Clare Shearer returns to Cardale, still fuming over the way Andrew kept her in the dark about David's affair, and wastes no time in claiming her share of the Beeches. Meanwhile, Joanna tries to make peace between a terminally ill woman and her daughter, but fears the rift may be too deep to heal. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Fiona Gillies

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Jonathan Paige is out celebrating his stag night when he is called to the woods where a terrified young man has been found bound and gagged _ a case which sparks memories of a previous child murder. As the wedding day approaches the couple find themselves deeper and deeper into the child abduction case and Cramer makes a life-changing decision. Nigel Havers stars as the medical sleuth with Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William's secretary calls in sick, so Hester volunteers to run the office in her place. However, when an important new client shows up for an appointment she finds herself having to fill in for her missing husband. Comedy, starring Anton Rogers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Harvey tries to cut staff costs by employing illegal immigrants, and soon has the residents up in arms. Tom decides he wants to be as fit as Linford Christie, but faces opposition from a concerned Diana. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole, Graham Crowden and Daniel Hill

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The wedding of Edith's mother Fanny to Monsieur Leclerc is brought forward to coincide with the execution of Michelle's most audacious plan to date

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: As preparations get under way for Compo's funeral, Clegg is amazed to be given a letter written by the old rascal with strict instructions that it should only be opened in event of his death. Peter Sallis, Kathy Staff and Frank Thornton star

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Three teenagers are shot dead while enjoying a picnic in a secluded wood. When a colleague of Wallander's is also murdered, he becomes convinced the two crimes are related, and sets out to catch the killer. However, the detective is faced with a lack of clues and an assassin who always seems to be one step ahead. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh and Sarah Smart

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the murder of a man found in an alleyway seven years previously when he is identified on a website covering unexplained disappearances. The victim is named by a woman whose husband hired him six months before his death, but neither of them had thought anything of it when their employee went missing - and seem to know little about him. Meanwhile, Brian and Gerry are distracted by looming job cuts and fear that Ucos could be downsized. With Robert Daws and Claudie Blakley

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The re-emergence of strike action brings a winter of discontent as firefighters, nurses, teachers, air traffic controllers, council workers and rail employees all clash with the Government. MI5 closely monitors the situation, aware of the inherent dangers - but when it discovers a section of the British Army is planning a walk-out of its own, it faces a battle to ensure the stability and security of the country. Reece Dinsdale guest stars, with Matthew Macfadyen and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of a member of the Jewish Hasidic community is discovered, and the team agrees to perform a 'dry' post mortem to respect the wishes of his faith. A forensic link is found to a group of Polish labourers, but Harry begins to wonder whether the victim's wife is hiding something, and decides to carry out a full autopsy to determine the time of death

programme.desc: Harvey invites local students into Bayview to interact with the residents, and bribes one to deliberately annoy troublemaking resident Diana in the hope she will retaliate and be thrown out of the home

programme.desc: General Von Klinkerhoffen is declared insane and taken to the hospital's psychiatric ward. Colonel Von Strohm, Herr Flick and Von Smallhausen become tied to a bomb in the sewer, while Fanny and Monsieur Le Clerc have problems on their wedding day

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo's funeral day arrives, and emotions run high as Clegg and Truly face the saddest moment of their lives. Elsewhere, others have problems deciding what to wear, and Howard suddenly finds himself torn between three demanding females. Long-running comedy starring Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mrs McCarthy's friend is the latest villager to die in her sleep, and Father Brown soon senses something sinister is afoot. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: Maisie's determination to help a couple find their missing daughter brings her into conflict with senior officers. Ignoring their objections she continues her investigations undaunted, but events take a dramatic turn as the full disturbing story gradually emerges. Police drama, starring Pauline Quirke

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the killing of a zookeeper, thought to have been mauled to death by a tiger in 2006, when blood found in the victim's lodgings suggests he was murdered before his body was placed in the big-cat enclosure. However, not all the team members are happy about the new case as Brian's animal rights tendencies come to the fore, bringing him into conflict with the zoo's PR. Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The team sets out to halt the progress of the Chala Cartel, a massive drug-trafficking system running from Bournemouth to Bali, after the deaths of eight Customs and Excise officers who were monitoring the criminals' activity. But when a leading British company is found to have links to the Colombians, the case takes a sinister turn as the possibility of corruption rears its ugly head. Matthew Macfadyen, Nicola Walker and Megan Dodds star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A boy's body is discovered near his school, and a search is launched when the classmate he was last seen with is reported missing. After it transpires the deceased was abused and has traces of an unknown toxic chemical in his body, the youngsters' headmaster, a man who used to teach Nikki, becomes a suspect - and a second corpse is found. Reece Dinsdale guest stars

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The local rugby team goes to France for a match. Vic finds his uncle's war grave, Simon has a fling with a French businesswoman, and Anna treats a boy for epilepsy

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe investigates a crooked takeaway whose staff he suspects of cooking up a burglary, but as he delves deeper into the case he finds an altogether more sinister operation. Crime drama, starring Richard Griffiths, Maggie Steed, Samantha Janus and Malcolm Sinclair

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Fisher comes under suspicion following a tragic accident during a pheasant shoot on a country estate, and asks Crabbe to investigate the case. Starring Richard Griffiths, Malcolm Sinclair and Ingrid Lacey

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Framed by long-time enemy Ducos for stealing Napoleon's treasures, Sharpe is abandoned by his wife Jane, and goes on the run from both the English and the French until he can prove his innocence. Period adventure, starring Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Abigail Cruttenden and Cecile Paoli

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Sontarans activate their masterplan and begin to choke Earth, while Unit is left defenceless, with a traitor in its ranks. As interplanetary war edges closer, the Doctor considers making the ultimate sacrifice to keep Martha and Donna alive. Freema Agyeman and Christopher Ryan guest star

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Tardis lands on the war-torn distant planet of Messaline, where a sample of the Doctor's DNA is used to create his clone, a fiesty young woman determined to defend her new home. While Donna and Martha get to know the newcomer, their time-travelling friend remains distant, and worries he may no longer be the last of the Time Lords. Guest starring Georgia Moffett, whose real-life father Peter Davison played the Doctor in the early 1980s

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Donna to 1926 where they meet Agatha Christie - on the day she famously disappeared for 10 days. A series of murders straight out of the writer's books unfolds, and the Doctor's investigation leads to a battle with a giant wasp. Guest starring Felicity Jones and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: When the beaten body of wealthy German businessman Gunter Schmeikel is discovered in the harbour, Gently and Bacchus are forced to consider whether a barman's anti-German sentiments are a motive for murder. However, the case takes a sinister turn when a key witness to Schmeikel's death is revealed to be a killer trained by Army Special Forces. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Tim Healy

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Alzheimer sufferer Edward Alderston comes under suspicion of involvement when an ex-finance company high-flyer responsible for the loss of many elderly people's life savings is found floating in a swimming pool with a samurai sword in his back

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne heads off for a quiet holiday at the seaside holiday town of Queenscliff, confident she will not be disturbed by an investigation. However, it is not long she and Jack discover buried treasure, tales of pirate ghosts - and murder. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Nathan Page

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to transform a Birmingham couple's garden, giving them a bird-filled place to relax with a fish pond and vegetable plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a fire-damaged silver vanity set, a secret agent canister from the Second World War and a wooden toy carousel. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat talks to Mitch again, wondering whether Gray found out about his lockdown conversations with Chantelle, when she told him how unhappy she was with her husband. But it only leaves Mitch frustrated and angry. Stacey has a heart-to-heart with Kat, worried that Jean will never forgive her for leaving. Ian does his hardest to avoid Max, blaming the bank for the delay in paying back his money, and Amy finds a way to still see Isaac on the way to school

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases, and reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell look at a terrace house in need of attention in Crewe, Cheshire, a characterful former woodman's cottage in Blackwell, West Midlands, and a small studio flat with a big city view in east London's Poplar. They then meet the new owners to hear their plans for their auction buys

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A drone goes for sale at a proceeds-of-crime auction, while police in Surrey target county-lines drug dealers. Plus, a look at how dedicated Trading Standards officers in Powys tracked down a cruel conman targeting the elderly. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Fraud specialists investigate a con-artist making thousands by pretending to know US politician Hillary Clinton. Plus, the race to stop £17million of unpaid tax going up in smoke. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as two teams search for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, led by experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks. Christina learns about 'The Leominster Chelsea Bun', which travelled to the battle fields of the First World War and back

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Events spiral out of Connie's control when her past catches up with her. Iris makes a shocking discovery, while Esther decides to face her critics, John receives bad news and Ellen tries to keep up her pretence with Richard. Drama, starring Seline Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how the seasonality of Britain's weather has forced people to devise ingenious ways of storing food through the winter months, revealing that tasty dishes have evolved from a need to preserve

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Couple Ian and Ruth run a 150-acre sheep farm in Rhayader, Powys, but don't have their own garden. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich find themselves called upon to create a Beatrix Potter-inspired one in an empty field on a budget of £2,500

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher experiences a blast from his past when he takes on restoring a vintage shop till, just like the ones he remembers from his childhood. Janine Savage from Dorset hopes wood aficionado Will Kirk can transform a very special dolls house custom made for her by her grandfather, while the Rev Richard Hayes arrives with a battered leather box containing a compact communion set, hoping its years of use can be made much less evident tghanks to the skills of Suzie Fletcher

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show, in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle it out for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Revamping their Norwich front garden has left Jane and Terry less than happy with their existing back garden, so the pair enlist Charlie Dimmock and the Harry and David Rich brothers to come up with a plan for a contemporary cottage garden. However, the budget they set for realising their dreams proves exacting to meet

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing champion Katherine Grainger, former Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson and decorated 400m runner Christine Ohuruogu join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray gets a visit from the police with new information. Ruby asks Martin if he still has feelings for Stacey, and Ian apologises to Sharon for taking his financial woes out on her. Amy refuses to go to school, and when Denise realises it's because of Isaac, the pre-teen makes an accusation against the teacher

programme.title: Michael McIntyre: Happy & Glorious
programme.desc: A chance to see a show from the popular stand-up star's 2015 tour of the UK and Ireland, recorded as-live at the O2 in London. Returning to a venue he has visited 27 times in the past, more than any other artist in the world, Michael showcases his particular brand of wit, taking aim once again at some of the absurdities of everyday life

programme.title: Old School (2003)
programme.desc: An embittered lawyer decides to relive the wild, crazy days of his youth after his girlfriend is unfaithful, and enlists the help of two equally disillusioned friends in setting up a fraternity house next to his former college campus. However, existing relationships are soon put under strain thanks to their activities, and the trio also face the wrath of the dean of the university, who is intent on getting them evicted in a bid to stop their actions. Comedy, starring Luke Wilson, Will Ferrell, Vince Vaughn and Juliette Lewis

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Freddy Bird, along with a special guest. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: In the third episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite savoury dishes, including a chicken and pink peppercorn pie, and simple, baked teriyaki noodles which are the perfect fuss-free midweek meal. Nadiya also demonstrates how to make a tear and share bread loaf with all the flavours of a pizza

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United and Sheffield Untied v Leeds United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss help two teams scour an antiques fair in Newark, Nottinghamshire, and Anita Manning learns about the rescue and restoration of a historic barge

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in rural Devon with an ambitious couple looking for a large country home and as much acreage as their £1.25million budget will afford them

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are called in by a young family in Fishponds, Bristol, to create a garden to unwind in after work. Zoe and Jonny are dedicated charity workers who want and need a sanctuary spot to relax away from their emotionally challenging work. They also want the designers to find a way of including a place for Zoe to grow her wedding bouquet for their upcoming marriage, somewhere for new baby, Ottilie, to play and even an outdoor spot for Zoe to practise alfresco yoga

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special comedians edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Ed Byrne, Jessie Cave, Dane Baptiste, Shazia Mirza, Angela Barnes, Rich Hall, Patrick Monahan and Junior Simpson

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, mother and daughter Julie and Lauren from Edinburgh, partners Ben and Adam from Staffordshire, and married couple Jess and John from Liverpool take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. In the first edition, Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade put together a boyband. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before being put through their paces alongside their competitors. Only the most impressive will make it through to the final line up to battle it out for the prize in the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly. This time, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable Strictly performances at Blackpool's famous ballroom, from the super-sized dances to the high-octane group numbers

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Marty reconnects with his homophobic father, and Rash is forced to intervene as disaster strikes the ED and the team are thrown into chaos. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and Neet Mohan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. United will have been looking for a repeat of their comfortable 3-0 victory here last term, while Chelsea won 4-0 the last time they visited the Baggies in November 2017

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a wall clock with an extraordinary story, a fractured lustre bowl and a semi acoustic guitar. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson transform an out-dated old garden into a luxurious outdoor space for Sarah and Tristan in Stevenage

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thousand-mile journey from the Swedish capital of Stockholm to Abisko in the northern reaches of the Arctic Circle. In Stockholm, Michael braves a precarious tour of the city from its rooftops before boarding a heritage tram to get the lowdown on 1930s Sweden from an expert. Moving on, he finishes his journey deep in the Arctic Circle at a remote climate research station. He also has lunch with traditional Sami people in Vilhelmina and checks into an ice hotel

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best able to answer questions. Susan Calman hosts

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin shows a businessman and a reprographic manager from Norfolk the tricks of the trade for buying and selling collectibles, before an auction showdown

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack has still not spoken to Carolyn, so Sarah decides she must do something to heal their wounds. Olivia returns from the Riviera with some unexpected news

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: As the end of the decade approaches, Sarah and George prepare to leave for his embassy posting in Israel, and Sarah tries one more time to reconcile Jack and Carolyn. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Inside the Perfect Predator
programme.desc: Documentary using ground-breaking computer graphics and close-up photography to examine four of the natural world's most extraordinary hunters. The programme follows a peregrine falcon on a mission to kill for its chicks, a great white shark hungry for seal blubber, a cheetah struggling to provide for its cubs and a Nile crocodile poised to ambush its dinner

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Charles Hanson are in Margam Country Park near Port Talbot, where items include a tea caddy and a book signed by David Livingstone. Paul Martin visits Cardiff Castle to see furniture by William Burges

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson test their knowledge in a series of games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Highland cattle farmers Simon and Emma are showing their finest bull Merlin at the Great Yorkshire Show. In the Shorthorn category, Stuart and Gail are taking a big step and showing their livestock for the first time. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: The River Wye with Will Millard
programme.desc: Writer and angler Will Millard sets out on a journey down the Wye from the Cambrian Mountains, going in search of the river's source on the slopes of Plynlimon before learning how a local sheep farmer has diversified by turning some of his land into a destination for petrolheads

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter ventures out from her home study, and her first stop is Stonehenge, a British cultural icon she has revered as a child. She has rare access to the stone circle where she meets leading cultural voices, including Turner Prize-winning artist Jeremy Deller and one of Britain's best-known actresses, Vanessa Redgrave. Mary also explores which cultural forms are the winners and losers of the pandemic

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the Wirral coast, the crew of Hoylake's rescue hovercraft is called out to an unconventional casualty - a porpoise stranded by the outgoing tide. Two hundred miles to the north another unusual victim of the British tides, a teenage wedding guest in suit and tie, is plucked off a fast-disappearing rocky outcrop in the middle of the sea by the Kinghorn crew, while in Cornwall, the Penlee lifeboat crew receives a dreaded call - a fishing boat, skippered by a fisherman the crew all know, is in flames miles out at sea

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A final selection of of highlights from the stand-up show, with Sean Lock, Katherine Ryan, Mawaan Rizwan, Holly Walsh, Andy Parsons, Lost Voice Guy and Darren Harriott offering their thoughts on the world of celebrity

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Having resigned from her job in PR, Julia tries to adapt to life as a freelancer and reluctantly agrees to help Amanda launch her new shop - even though she believes it is nothing but a vanity project. The opening is a success, but a careless online post threatens to destroy her good work. Meanwhile, Liz rails against the gentrification of the area and the loss of her beloved kebab shop, while Kevin reveals he has written an unusual children's book, which he wants Amanda to sell in her shop

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Joining him in an attempt to make sense of the world around them are Sara Pascoe, Sophie Duker, Jamali Maddix, Jen Brister and Dane Baptiste

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Comedy short featuring Bob Mortimer and Frankie Boyle. Frankie shows Bob his recipe for Lancashire Hot Pot, but runs into trouble when the show fails to deliver its quota of fun, education and social media buzz.

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An alternative nature documentary following the life and struggles of wild wolves as they attempt to hunt down a herd of elk. Narrated by Matt Berry and full of fruity language

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Comedy short set in the 1930s in which Hilda Stolf (Sally Phillips) visits her doctor (Reece Shearsmith) complaining of a rare case of 'high foot'

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian explores the history of cookery shows, taking a look back at memorable moments and mishaps from programmes featuring Fanny Cradock, Delia Smith, Mary Berry, Jamie Oliver and Ainsley Harriott. He also recalls the highlights of MasterChef and The Great British Bake Off, and reveals why innuendo is an essential element of a great cooking show

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: A new redevelopment scheme is launched on the edge of the city centre, intended to create nearly 15,000 new properties, but leaving many residents afraid their homes will be demolished to make way for tower blocks. Among those at risk is a woman who is caring for her elderly bed-bound father, who urgently needs to know how long they can stay in the family home. At the West Tower, interior designer Sarah works on a penthouse for a millionaire client, spending thousands of pounds on bespoke furniture

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: Ashley John-Baptiste meets a group of people working to clean up their neighbourhood, and Rev Kate Bottley goes in search of inner peace on a wire high above the cliffs of Anglesey

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford finds out if a daily glass of wine is a good thing and Chris Bavin asks if calorie counting is a sensible approach to weight loss

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a fire-damaged silver vanity set, a secret agent canister from the Second World War and a wooden toy carousel. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to transform a Birmingham couple's garden, giving them a bird-filled place to relax with a fish pond and vegetable plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team sets to work restoring four treasured family heirlooms. Metal worker Dom Chinea takes on the challenge of restoring an old, dilapidated barber's pole, while luthier David Kennett turns his attention to a broken Portuguese guitar. Elsewhere, Lucia Scalisi takes on the restoration of a Nigerian painting, and Will Kirk transforms a wooden writing slope that hid a life-changing secret

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie. A team of three contestants faces a golden wall of cash worth £20,000 and its members try to keep it intact as much of it as possible by correctly answering a series of questions

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Coverage of the elite men's time-trial in the annual event, which this year takes place in Imola, Italy. Great Britain's Geraint Thomas is among those going for gold in the event won by Australian Rohan Dennis in each of the last two years

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Romulus Whitaker, a snake expert with more than 40 years of field experience, sets out to discover the truth behind a reported rise in snake bites in India, which may have caused more human deaths than the official statistics suggest. He investigates some of the subcontinent's deadliest reptiles, including the king cobra and saw-scaled viper, and explores the natural history that has led to this epidemic

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth meeting of the season, which comes from Qatar Sports Club. Great Britain's Dina Asher-Smith was victorious in 200m in this meeting last year, while there were also impressive wins for the likes of South Africa's Caster Semenya in the 800m and Kenya's Hellen Obiri in the 3,000m and Soufiane El Bakkali in the 3,00m steeplechase

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown, the Rev Kate Bottley, Joel Dommett and John Thomson go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kelly Brook and Denise Van Outen compete in the cross-country bargain-hunting challenge, setting out on a trip around south-east England in classic cars. They receive expert guidance from Charles Hanson and Tim Medhurst as they visit antique shops and emporiums to find items to sell at auction

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost celebrates some of the glorious gardens the programme has visited over the last few years and gives plenty of seasonal hints and tips for viewers to be doing in our own gardens. Frances Tophill visits a lochside retreat, Joe Swift finds lots of take home tips from a city garden, Carol Klein is inspired by plant combinations in West Yorkshire and Advolly Richmond shows how a historical landscape was designed for fun and frolic. Plus, the couple who let nature be their gardening guide and a gardener who makes containers her focus of attention

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian explores how the Romantics created the secular cult of national belonging in their poetry, music and art. In doing so, he explores the genesis of modern nationalism - its Romantic roots in a new idea of nature and homeland; the 'discovery' of native folklore, song and stories and above all the part played by music - especially Frédéric Chopin's mazurkas - in generating the emotion of nationalism. Contributors include singer-songwriter Eddi Reader, German artist Mariele Neudecker and pianist Piotr Anderszewski

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The musician opens the doors to his unique south London recording studio, joined by an esteemed guest to discuss their musical journey, loves and influences. Their discussion is illustrated by classic clips from the archive, supplemented by some unique new performances from during the current restrictions. Beginning the series is this special episode featuring the winner of the much-coveted Mercury Prize 2020

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Kara is announced missing after being shot down in an enemy attack, so Lee and Commander Adama insist they must do whatever is necessary to find and rescue her - even if it means they use up precious resources, which could jeopardise the entire fleet

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Billy holds his first press conference for the President, while Sharon and Tyrol slip away for a tryst. Towards the end of the day a suicide bomber slips through Galactica's security, but just as Colonel Tigh spots him, he blows himself up

programme.title: The Midwife (2017)
programme.desc: Claire works as a midwife at a maternity hospital that is soon to be shut down. One day, her late father's former mistress, whom Claire resents for having abandoned him shortly before he died, decides to come back into her life. Drama, starring Catherine Deneuve, Catherine Frot and Olivier Gourmet. In French

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Richard Bilton uncovers secret reports that expose how banks have failed to tackle crime and how terrorists, money launderers and crime bosses are able to use the same banks as ordinary citizens. He also reveals the double life of the man who funded the Brexit Party, secret deals at the top of British football and the business deals billionaires would rather you didn't know about

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker reflects on 25 years of his documentaries, featuring brand new conversations between Louis and some of his most memorable contributors. In this first edition, he looks back at his earliest documentaries, and explores how some people's most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society. Louis catches up with some of his previous contributors including Mike Cain, who he first met in the mid-90s, having moved to the hills of Idaho for what he saw as an inevitable war with the federal government

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine footage of strange wildlife, starting with trees covered in spider webs and bees that make blue, red, and green honey

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a den for 11-year-old aspiring journalist Soibi, who is finding it hard to write up her reports in the small bedroom she shares with her three-year-old sister

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends compete in Willaston's World Worm Charming Championships.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Three badge holders meet David Attenborough and Dippy the Dinosaur embarks on a tour

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve goes on a mission to track down the deadliest of all dinosaur defenders. He meets the armoured tank that is Nodosaurus, tests whether an Iguanodon really has what it takes to beat a T-Rex, and - thanks to some mega engineering - unleashes an Ankylosaurus tail-swinging robot that goes on the rampage

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox continues his exploration of the origins of mankind by investigating the matter from which everything on Earth is formed. At a Hindu cremation in Nepal, he discusses the faith's philosophy of life as an eternal cycle of creation and destruction, and draws a parallel with the lives of the universe's stars - before revealing why everything on Earth was once a part of something else

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham prepare for a visit from the vet, who is coming to take blood samples from the herd to test for a chronic wasting disease. In Northumberland, Emma's six-month-old puppy Grey is introduced to sheep for the first time, while in the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair are dealing with an unexpected new arrival. On Little Rowater Farm in Aberdeenshire, a deer calf shows signs of being bullied by the herd

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: In the vast wilderness of Canada's west coast lie forests that are home to North American black bears that through a quirk of genetics are born pure white and known as ghost bears. Canadian film-maker Jeff Turner and his own family spend a year following a she-bear and its cubs as they coped with the constant threat of hunger and potential predators all around

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Live T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the third match of the five-game series, held at Incora County Ground in Derby. The tourists will be looking for revenge in this series after England beat them by 46 runs to reach the T20 World Cup semi-finals in March

programme.title: Live Women's FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Arsenal v Tottenham Hotspur (Kick-off 5.15pm). Coverage of the quarter-final between the London rivals, which takes place at Meadow Park in Boreham Wood. Arsenal have won this competition a record five times and will start this match as favourites. However, Spurs made a big statement this month when they signed American star Alex Morgan and will be looking to prove they are now a match for their neighbours

programme.title: Bernard Haitink, the Enigmatic Maestro
programme.desc: After conducting for 65 years, Bernard Haitink retired at the age of 90 in September 2019. The musicians he has worked with are puzzled by the secrets of his technique. He himself says his job is to embrace the orchestra without suffocating. Here, documentary-maker John Bridcut follows the Dutch maestro in his final year of music-making, watching him at work and talking to those who have worked alongside him throughout his long career

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the high-brow quiz, serving up a selection of questions on the subject of restaurants. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Phill Jupitus, Sara Pascoe and Mark Watson

programme.title: McQueen (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary charting the life and career of uncompromising fashion designer Alexander McQueen. Through a combination of archive footage and interviews with family, friends and colleagues, including Kate Moss, Jodie Kidd and Tom Ford, the film charts McQueen's story, taking in his childhood in the East End, his start as a tailor, launching and overseeing his eponymous line and his untimely death

programme.title: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas (2008)
programme.desc: Bruno, a boy in Nazi Germany, moves to the country when his father is placed in charge of a concentration camp. While exploring the woods close to the facility's boundary, he spots a Jewish youngster being held there, and the two become friends - but Bruno remains innocently oblivious to the camp's true purpose. Drama, starring David Thewlis, Asa Butterfield and Jack Scanlon

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earnest 'Earn' Marks is broke, out of luck and living with his ex-girlfriend and their daughter, so when he learns that his cousin Alfred is on the verge of rap success under the name of Paper Boi, he sees an opportunity for both of them. First episode of the comedy drama, starring Donald Glover

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: The cousins are arrested after their shooting incident and while Paper Boi posts bail quickly, Earn is left at the station waiting to be processed along with everyone else who was arrested overnight. Meanwhile, the rapper is released to find that the incident has boosted his popularity

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Earn spends all his money to get Paper Boi's song played on the radio, leaving him with no cash for his date with Van. Meanwhile, Alfred and Darius get deeper into the drugs trade

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Emily's keepers consider sending her back into the wild when she becomes irritable and overly dominant. Michaela Strachan meets the newest arrival in the sanctuary, happy baby Jipe, and Jonathan Scott follows a recently released herd of 10, which is attacked by a rabid dog

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints an ice-cold pond surrounded by snow-covered banks, with soft hues in the sky

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Lean's epic drama based on Boris Pasternak's novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about Anne Hamilton-Byrne, a beautiful, charismatic and delusional woman who created an apocalyptic Australian sect dubbed The Family. With her husband Bill, she acquired numerous children - some through adoption scams, some born to cult members - and raised them as her own

programme.title: David Stratton's Stories of Australian Cinema
programme.desc: The critic tells the story of Australian movie-making, focusing on the films that capture this idiosyncratic nation with drama, emotion and humour. This episode sees how Aussie film-makers gained the confidence to tell their own stories with the boldness of Picnic at Hanging Rock and Mad Max, the global success of Crocodile Dundee and Shine, the flamboyance of Strictly Ballroom and the raw authenticity of Samson and Delilah

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director of Shaun of the Dead, Baby Driver and Hot Fuzz talks to Robbie Collin about the movies that have influenced his career. His choices range from An American Werewolf in London, Carrie and Bullitt to Mad Max: Fury Road and 1930s musical Dames

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores archaeological finds across the North of Britain. A team discovers clues to Scotland's first kingdoms, metal detectorists unearth a hoard of Viking treasure and a new housing development reveals a graveyard of Iron Age warriors

programme.title: Elephant Diaries
programme.desc: Jonathan Scott visits northern Tsavo to determine the extent of the rabies outbreak and the fate of the orphaned elephants, then hurries south to see Emily before she is released back into the wild. In Nairobi, Michaela Strachan reports on babies Jipe and Late, and takes part in an extraordinary football match, where the orphans compete against the keepers

programme.title: Top of the Pops: Kylie Minogue
programme.desc: A compilation of classic Kylie performances from the Top of the Pops studio. First shown in 1998

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1981 Big Hits
programme.desc: The year 1981 embraced disco and ska, new wave punk, the burgeoning New Romantic scene and the rise of synthpop, with some prog quirkiness and good old rock'n'roll thrown in. Performances from big hitter soloists Phil Collins, Shakin' Stevens and Kim Wilde are featured alongside the exuberant chaos of groups like Tenpole Tudor, Adam and the Ants and the Teardrop Explodes. Plus, Odyssey, Clare Grogan, the Specials and the Human League

programme.title: Australian Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at a wide-ranging selection of songs and music acts that came from Down Under, with clips of their appearances on some top UK shows. Featured chartbusters include pop pioneers the Easybeats, rock 'n' roll legends AC/DC, and INXS who became one of the nation's most successful acts of all time. Also included are ground-breaking Aborigine band Yothu Yindi, and the more famiiliar faces of Nick Cave, John Farnham, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan

programme.title: Mystify: Michael Hutchence (2019)
programme.desc: Profile of the INXS singer, featuring footage from private home movies and contributions by his friends and family to delve beneath the public persona of the charismatic frontman. The film also examines the traumatic brain injury he suffered in 1992, which caused him to lose his senses of smell and taste, along with the onslaught of tabloid press scrutiny due to his relationship with Paula Yates in the mid-1990s, before his death in November 1997

programme.title: INXS: Live Baby Live
programme.desc: A newly remastered version of the group's performance at Wembley Stadium in July 1991, directed by David Mallet. The concert captures the Australian rockers - led by charismatic frontman Michael Hutchence - at the peak of their powers, and features classic anthems including New Sensation, Mystify, Need You Tonight and Never Tear Us Apart

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers

programme.title: Australian Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at a wide-ranging selection of songs and music acts that came from Down Under, with clips of their appearances on some top UK shows. Featured chartbusters include pop pioneers the Easybeats, rock 'n' roll legends AC/DC, and INXS who became one of the nation's most successful acts of all time. Also included are ground-breaking Aborigine band Yothu Yindi, and the more famiiliar faces of Nick Cave, John Farnham, Kylie Minogue and Jason Donovan

programme.title: Top of the Pops: Kylie Minogue
programme.desc: A compilation of classic Kylie performances from the Top of the Pops studio. First shown in 1998

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers gives their reflections on what each season means to them. In autumn, Argyll, Sybil and George gather their sheep from over six square miles of mountainside, while in the east of Scotland, Mel and Martin buy rams for their flock. In spring, Martin collects semen from his prize bull, John deals with an emergency caesarean, and Mel fights to save a poorly lamb's life. As summer arrives, the farmers can finally put their cattle in the fields, with Martin's having been indoors for eight months

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin goes on safari in Kenya's Masai Mara. His journey continues through Tanzania to Ujiji - and on to the place where Victorian explorers Stanley and Livingstone became acquainted. Sailing down Lake Tanganyika in a dugout canoe, he anticipates a fairly straightforward route through Zambia, Zimbabwe and South Africa in the hope he will arrive in time to catch the annual boat trip from Cape Town to Antarctica

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Rid of the human remains, Sandra's dig is disturbed by an intolerant tribal elder, while Jay presses Simon on his suspicions

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay refuses to accept that the drugs case has been solved. Meanwhile, the forensics on the body in the bag add to Fran's distress

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Paris
programme.desc: In the 1960s as a young expatriate Australian writer, Clive James discovered Paris, which he felt was his spiritual home. In this programme he returns to the city and meets the writers, the models and the actresses who were part of a different world in his youth, and also encounters the unchanging arch-enemy of any visitor - the Paris taxi driver

programme.title: So Awkward
programme.desc: Sid has an app which flags local eco protests. He and Jas sign up to a bike ride organised to draw attention to climate change. When Sid discovers that participants are expected to shave their heads after the ride (to symbolise change can happen fast), he has an identity crisis: Good Sid wants to shave his head to support the cause but Bad Sid does not want to be bald and wants to pull out of the race. Mr Malone plans to leave school early to watch the Men’s Netball World Cup Final at home. He arranges for Martha and Ollie to teach his Year 7 class and ensures Sid will boycott a tutorial by telling him that his netball mascot ThunderBun is made out of non-biodegradable polyester. But Sid decides to chain himself to Mr Malone’s desk instead to avoid having his head shaved. programme.desc: Set 20 years in the future, Endlings is the empowering story of four teenagers in foster care who discover they're not alone in the universe, even though sometimes it can feel like they are. Halfway across the world, Tuko is alone. After the death of her mate, Tuko becomes the last elephant in existence--what biologists call an endling. Her endling status summons to Earth a mysterious Alien who is on an intergalactic quest to save the last member of near-extinct species. After Tuko is collected, the Alien's ship malfunctions and sends it on a crash course with the farm where Julia (Kamaia Fairburn), Johnny (Edison Grant), Tabby (Michela Luci) and Finn (Cale Thomas Ferrin) live with their foster dad, Mr. Leopold (Neil Crone). Before crashing, the Alien releases all of its cosmic collection into the fields and wilderness surrounding the property. She flew in the space shuttle three times, she delivered Hubble into orbit and was the first American woman to walk in space!
