programme.desc: The youngster and her mother hear some church bells, so they decide to investigate and on entering a church meet some bell ringers.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: The pals admire Patchwork Bird, and want to look just as pretty. Patchwork Fox is an exception, as he just has a pretty good appetite

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley helps make lavender oil and JB Gill discovers how pigs keep cool in the summer. Plus, a look at how blackcurrants are made into juice

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: Tyra helps mum cook lunch in Ghana, while in Peru, Angel plays football with his cousins

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie is visited by Grandad and Grandma on the farm. They help Ottalie pick apples from the tree, before Ottalie and Grandma get to work making a tasty apple sponge for pudding

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Lily and Felix explain to Hugo all the different ways people are kind to one another, before Hugo gives almost all of his balloons away to his friends

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory Crawford isits Lily and her cat, Brody, and they clean out her litter tray and feed her together, before having lots of cuddles. Later, they head to the cat grooming parlour

programme.title: Monty & Co
programme.desc: Lulu feels ignored and gets into trouble, so she hides away. The others worry that she has run off.

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: The Leafs take refuge in the Olobobs's home, but like it so much that they won't leave

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is going on an adventure in his garden. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends are at an adventure park looking for animals, climbing and grass sledging!

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: A journey back 65 million years to the time of the Tyrannosaurus Rex and its existence in North America

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate finds out how wearing glasses helps people to see more clearly. She visits an optician to see how an eye test works by having one herself. She reads the charts and shapes on the wall and looks into a special camera that takes a picture of the back of our eyes to make sure they are healthy. Then, Maddie shows us how glasses frames are made by shaping gold wire at a factory. Maddie uses a special microscope camera to show us the ant-sized screws that hold glasses together!

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter realises he has accidentally upset Benjamin by asking him to sit out the next garden raid. However, he finds that his cousin has gone when he goes to apologise

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: But even though it's sunny when they leave the house, a rainy weather forecast threatens her plans. Luckily for her, Gran Gran has come prepared!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's teacher comes for a home visit and the Brooklyn-Bells want Waffle to be on his best behaviour, but Waffle cannot stop chewing everything

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: ory visits Eva and her pet cat Chewie, who is going to the vet to check his weight. Afterwards they take him home to try new ways to help him lose weight. Ferne joins a vet at a wildlife hospital and finds out how they care for local wildlife

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a place where they'll make a nature collage using leaves and sticks. On the way, they discover a chocolate river and a bridge that looks like a sheep, choo-choo like an old train and even meet a dragon!

programme.title: At Home with Mr Tumble
programme.desc: Mr Tumble is at home, pretending to be an astronaut.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Pink Bird runs wild in a department store, buying lots of beautiful things

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari asks her friend Barry how he came to be so good at windsurfing, and discovers that the secret to developing any skill is to keep on trying - even when you fail

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African folk tale that reveals why hummingbirds hum

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to Indonesia in search of the world's largest lizard, the Komodo dragon. Along the way, he takes to skies with a draco lizard and gets help from a water buffalo

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly organises a sleepover at the Big Hub to raise money to fix the leaky roof, but when everyone gathers that night Magnus reveals he is scared of the dark

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: The school's teacher is retiring, which worries Katie, on top of news that her mum and dad are having another baby

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady's poetry records inspire a snowy train ride to the mountains

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The children accidentally sell Charlie's goggles to Morton, who then refuses to swap them back

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy sets up her own special megaphone to sing. Unfortunately, there is so much noise coming from the Tombliboos' house that she cannot make herself heard

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Liz Carr reads I Like Myself, by Karen Beaumont and David Catrow

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a fire-damaged house in West Sussex, a semi-detached in the West Midlands and a barn conversion in Kent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Merseyside Police investigate a carer believed to have taken a huge sum of money from the person she was looking after. Also, a fraudster's designer clothes are put up for auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The fraud investigators hunt a reckless hoaxer faking accidents for fun and a random road-side stop unearths the UK's biggest Blue Badge fraud

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value at Nottingham racecourse to take to auction. Anita also visits the Museum of Timekeeping, near Newark, to find out about a clock that kept millions punctual

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Channing refuses to tell Ellen he has been seeing Esther at the manor, and John decides to reveal the truth to Joyce. Danny threatens to hurt Henry if Connie does not help with his planned robbery, while Frank has a fight with Walter when he learns Iris is clearing dead rats from the barn

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King explore the history behind the boom in pub food, tracing it from the humble beginnings of bar snacks and chicken-in-a-basket to the birth of the gastro pub. The duo also recreate some mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunting couple find a country home with a budget of £700,000 in Buckinghamshire, and later visits a farm that produces high-end snail caviar

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to transform a Birmingham couple's garden, giving them a bird-filled place to relax with a fish pond and vegetable plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a fire-damaged silver vanity set, a secret agent canister from the Second World War and a wooden toy carousel. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat talks to Mitch again, wondering whether Gray found out about his lockdown conversations with Chantelle, when she told him how unhappy she was with her husband. But it only leaves Mitch frustrated and angry. Stacey has a heart-to-heart with Kat, worried that Jean will never forgive her for leaving. Ian does his hardest to avoid Max, blaming the bank for the delay in paying back his money, and Amy finds a way to still see Isaac on the way to school

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases, and reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell look at a terrace house in need of attention in Crewe, Cheshire, a characterful former woodman's cottage in Blackwell, West Midlands, and a small studio flat with a big city view in east London's Poplar. They then meet the new owners to hear their plans for their auction buys

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A drone goes for sale at a proceeds-of-crime auction, while police in Surrey target county-lines drug dealers. Plus, a look at how dedicated Trading Standards officers in Powys tracked down a cruel conman targeting the elderly. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Fraud specialists investigate a con-artist making thousands by pretending to know US politician Hillary Clinton. Plus, the race to stop £17million of unpaid tax going up in smoke. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as two teams search for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, led by experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks. Christina learns about 'The Leominster Chelsea Bun', which travelled to the battle fields of the First World War and back

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Events spiral out of Connie's control when her past catches up with her. Iris makes a shocking discovery, while Esther decides to face her critics, John receives bad news and Ellen tries to keep up her pretence with Richard. Drama, starring Seline Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how the seasonality of Britain's weather has forced people to devise ingenious ways of storing food through the winter months, revealing that tasty dishes have evolved from a need to preserve

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Couple Ian and Ruth run a 150-acre sheep farm in Rhayader, Powys, but don't have their own garden. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich find themselves called upon to create a Beatrix Potter-inspired one in an empty field on a budget of £2,500

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher experiences a blast from his past when he takes on restoring a vintage shop till, just like the ones he remembers from his childhood. Janine Savage from Dorset hopes wood aficionado Will Kirk can transform a very special dolls house custom made for her by her grandfather, while the Rev Richard Hayes arrives with a battered leather box containing a compact communion set, hoping its years of use can be made much less evident tghanks to the skills of Suzie Fletcher

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show, in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle it out for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: Welsh slate once roofed the buildings of the British Empire and by the late 1800s a third of all roofing slates on the planet came from north Wales. The industry produced communities, culture and an identity that are still alive today - but much of the history has been forgotten or vanished. Will travels to the region to track down some of the remains and relics from this vibrant period of industry by exploring deep inside the mountains of Snowdonia in some of biggest slate mines in the world

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing champion Katherine Grainger, former Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson and decorated 400m runner Christine Ohuruogu join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray gets a visit from the police with new information. Ruby asks Martin if he still has feelings for Stacey, and Ian apologises to Sharon for taking his financial woes out on her. Amy refuses to go to school, and when Denise realises it's because of Isaac, the pre-teen makes an accusation against the teacher

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Guest panellists Jack Dee, Romesh Ranganathan, Gaby Roslin and Tinchy Stryder join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a coach tour guide, escorting a busload of pensioners to Antwerp and Bruges. His talent with an audience proves of little use when he has to reel off a list of facts about Belgium, and he finds even getting up early every morning is major challenge

programme.title: Michael McIntyre: Happy & Glorious
programme.desc: A chance to see a show from the popular stand-up star's 2015 tour of the UK and Ireland, recorded as-live at the O2 in London. Returning to a venue he has visited 27 times in the past, more than any other artist in the world, Michael showcases his particular brand of wit, taking aim once again at some of the absurdities of everyday life

programme.title: Old School (2003)
programme.desc: An embittered lawyer decides to relive the wild, crazy days of his youth after his girlfriend is unfaithful, and enlists the help of two equally disillusioned friends in setting up a fraternity house next to his former college campus. However, existing relationships are soon put under strain thanks to their activities, and the trio also face the wrath of the dean of the university, who is intent on getting them evicted in a bid to stop their actions. Comedy, starring Luke Wilson, Will Ferrell, Vince Vaughn and Juliette Lewis

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Freddy Bird, along with a special guest. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: In the third episode, Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite savoury dishes, including a chicken and pink peppercorn pie, and simple, baked teriyaki noodles which are the perfect fuss-free midweek meal. Nadiya also demonstrates how to make a tear and share bread loaf with all the flavours of a pizza

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United and Sheffield Untied v Leeds United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss help two teams scour an antiques fair in Newark, Nottinghamshire, and Anita Manning learns about the rescue and restoration of a historic barge

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in rural Devon with an ambitious couple looking for a large country home and as much acreage as their £1.25million budget will afford them

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages an old desk, a pink armchair, a rusty old bucket and an unwanted painting, handing them out for renovation before giving any profits to the owners

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway treks from Harlech to Barmouth, stripping down to basics as he crosses a nudist beach in Gwynedd. On this walk along one of Wales' most popular coastlines, Derek also rolls a cheese down the world's steepest hill and learns about the 'Devil in the Shawl' when he visits an old village chapel. Along his journey, he nearly gets caught out on a tidal causeway and meets a team of women who give up their free time to keep the beaches clean and safe for wildlife

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special comedians edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Ed Byrne, Jessie Cave, Dane Baptiste, Shazia Mirza, Angela Barnes, Rich Hall, Patrick Monahan and Junior Simpson

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, mother and daughter Julie and Lauren from Edinburgh, partners Ben and Adam from Staffordshire, and married couple Jess and John from Liverpool take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. In the first edition, Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade put together a boyband. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before being put through their paces alongside their competitors. Only the most impressive will make it through to the final line up to battle it out for the prize in the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly. This time, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable Strictly performances at Blackpool's famous ballroom, from the super-sized dances to the high-octane group numbers

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Marty reconnects with his homophobic father, and Rash is forced to intervene as disaster strikes the ED and the team are thrown into chaos. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and Neet Mohan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. United will have been looking for a repeat of their comfortable 3-0 victory here last term, while Chelsea won 4-0 the last time they visited the Baggies in November 2017

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Laura Goodwin

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: In a seasonal special, the Landward team celebrates one of the busiest and most exciting times in the countryside calendar - harvest. Dougie Vipond finds out about a new product making use of an ancient crop, Anne Lundon meets the farming family whose members have cut out the middle man to sell their product direct to brewers and Arlene Stuart looks at how the pandemic has affected the market for farmers' crops. Plus, Euan Mcilwraith meets the folk keeping the old ways alive with a vintage harvest and there's a look at a new hydrogen-powered tractor

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing champion Katherine Grainger, former Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson and decorated 400m runner Christine Ohuruogu join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray gets a visit from the police with new information. Ruby asks Martin if he still has feelings for Stacey, and Ian apologises to Sharon for taking his financial woes out on her. Amy refuses to go to school, and when Denise realises it's because of Isaac, the pre-teen makes an accusation against the teacher

programme.title: Michael McIntyre: Happy & Glorious
programme.desc: A chance to see a show from the popular stand-up star's 2015 tour of the UK and Ireland, recorded as-live at the O2 in London. Returning to a venue he has visited 27 times in the past, more than any other artist in the world, Michael showcases his particular brand of wit, taking aim once again at some of the absurdities of everyday life

programme.title: Old School (2003)
programme.desc: An embittered lawyer decides to relive the wild, crazy days of his youth after his girlfriend is unfaithful, and enlists the help of two equally disillusioned friends in setting up a fraternity house next to his former college campus. However, existing relationships are soon put under strain thanks to their activities, and the trio also face the wrath of the dean of the university, who is intent on getting them evicted in a bid to stop their actions. Comedy, starring Luke Wilson, Will Ferrell, Vince Vaughn and Juliette Lewis

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

Nadiya also demonstrates how to make a tear and share bread loaf with all the flavours of a pizza

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United and Sheffield Untied v Leeds United

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in rural Devon with an ambitious couple looking for a large country home and as much acreage as their £1.25million budget will afford them

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are called in by a young family in Fishponds, Bristol, to create a garden to unwind in after work. Zoe and Jonny are dedicated charity workers who want and need a sanctuary spot to relax away from their emotionally challenging work. They also want the designers to find a way of including a place for Zoe to grow her wedding bouquet for their upcoming marriage, somewhere for new baby, Ottilie, to play and even an outdoor spot for Zoe to practise alfresco yoga

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to design duo Josh and Oli to repurpose an old mower, while upholsterer Leigh-Anne Treadwell goes out on a limb with her redesign of a pair of teak chairs. Sarah's challenge is to find new homes for three Persian rugs. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special comedians edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Ed Byrne, Jessie Cave, Dane Baptiste, Shazia Mirza, Angela Barnes, Rich Hall, Patrick Monahan and Junior Simpson

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the return of the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In the first episode, mother and daughter Julie and Lauren from Edinburgh, partners Ben and Adam from Staffordshire, and married couple Jess and John from Liverpool take part

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. In the first edition, Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade put together a boyband. The talented hopefuls are auditioned individually, before being put through their paces alongside their competitors. Only the most impressive will make it through to the final line up to battle it out for the prize in the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly. This time, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable Strictly performances at Blackpool's famous ballroom, from the super-sized dances to the high-octane group numbers

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Hospital drama with the staff of Holby's accident and emergency department as they deal with more sick and injured patients. Marty reconnects with his homophobic father, and Rash is forced to intervene as disaster strikes the ED and the team are thrown into chaos. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and Neet Mohan

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester United at Amex Stadium and West Bromwich Albion v Chelsea at The Hawthorns. United will have been looking for a repeat of their comfortable 3-0 victory here last term, while Chelsea won 4-0 the last time they visited the Baggies in November 2017

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland introduces highlights of Saturday's Scottish Premiership matches. Dundee United visited Hamilton Academical, Livingston were at home to St Johnstone and St Mirren hosted Kilmarnock

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell discuss the latest talking points from the NFL and look ahead to the weekend's action

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a fire-damaged house in West Sussex, a semi-detached in the West Midlands and a barn conversion in Kent

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Merseyside Police investigate a carer believed to have taken a huge sum of money from the person she was looking after. Also, a fraudster's designer clothes are put up for auction

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: The fraud investigators hunt a reckless hoaxer faking accidents for fun and a random road-side stop unearths the UK's biggest Blue Badge fraud

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts, as experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell help two teams find items of value at Nottingham racecourse to take to auction. Anita also visits the Museum of Timekeeping, near Newark, to find out about a clock that kept millions punctual

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Channing refuses to tell Ellen he has been seeing Esther at the manor, and John decides to reveal the truth to Joyce. Danny threatens to hurt Henry if Connie does not help with his planned robbery, while Frank has a fight with Walter when he learns Iris is clearing dead rats from the barn

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King explore the history behind the boom in pub food, tracing it from the humble beginnings of bar snacks and chicken-in-a-basket to the birth of the gastro pub. The duo also recreate some mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a house hunting couple find a country home with a budget of £700,000 in Buckinghamshire, and later visits a farm that produces high-end snail caviar

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Arit Anderson compete for the chance to transform a Birmingham couple's garden, giving them a bird-filled place to relax with a fish pond and vegetable plot

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on the restoration of a fire-damaged silver vanity set, a secret agent canister from the Second World War and a wooden toy carousel. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan host the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kheerat talks to Mitch again, wondering whether Gray found out about his lockdown conversations with Chantelle, when she told him how unhappy she was with her husband. But it only leaves Mitch frustrated and angry. Stacey has a heart-to-heart with Kat, worried that Jean will never forgive her for leaving. Ian does his hardest to avoid Max, blaming the bank for the delay in paying back his money, and Amy finds a way to still see Isaac on the way to school

programme.title: The Grand Party Hotel
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Shankly Hotel in Liverpool, which has themed party suites so guests can celebrate important moments in their lives. But with the hotel's online rating having slipped, a new general manager has been called in to shake things up, although his changes do not go down well with the staff. Elsewhere, wedding planner Ciara helps brides-to-be Trudie and Danielle, while guest relations officer Liam looks after Laura, who has checked in to celebrate her recent divorce

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie mayoral elections are thrown into disarray when one of the candidates is stabbed to death in the polling booth while casting his vote. Can DI Jack Mooney and his team get to the bottom of this political puzzle? Ardal O'Hanlon stars

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Carbohydrates are at the heart of most people's diet, but with 63 per cent of all UK adults now considered obese, many experts believe people are eating the wrong sort of carbohydrate. Dr Xand van Tulleken sets out to discover whether they really are to blame. He learns how much sugar a selection of common foods releases into the bloodstream, investigates whether bread and pasta really are 'bad' foods and teams up with a Merseyside GP to trial a healthy eating plan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley ask for viewers' help with unsolved cases, and reporter John-Paul Davies is out and about with stories from across the UK

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell look at a terrace house in need of attention in Crewe, Cheshire, a characterful former woodman's cottage in Blackwell, West Midlands, and a small studio flat with a big city view in east London's Poplar. They then meet the new owners to hear their plans for their auction buys

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A drone goes for sale at a proceeds-of-crime auction, while police in Surrey target county-lines drug dealers. Plus, a look at how dedicated Trading Standards officers in Powys tracked down a cruel conman targeting the elderly. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Fraud specialists investigate a con-artist making thousands by pretending to know US politician Hillary Clinton. Plus, the race to stop £17million of unpaid tax going up in smoke. Presented by Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion presents as two teams search for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, led by experts Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks. Christina learns about 'The Leominster Chelsea Bun', which travelled to the battle fields of the First World War and back

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Events spiral out of Connie's control when her past catches up with her. Iris makes a shocking discovery, while Esther decides to face her critics, John receives bad news and Ellen tries to keep up her pretence with Richard. Drama, starring Seline Hizli

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how the seasonality of Britain's weather has forced people to devise ingenious ways of storing food through the winter months, revealing that tasty dishes have evolved from a need to preserve

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple of empty nesters with a £425,000 budget, who wish to relocate to rural Wiltshire in a bid to enjoy more wildlife and outdoor pursuits. While there, Jonnie goes underground and learns more about the secret intelligence activities of the Auxiliary Unit during the Second World War. This intriguing operation was masterminded by Captain Peter Fleming, brother of Bond author Ian Fleming

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Couple Ian and Ruth run a 150-acre sheep farm in Rhayader, Powys, but don't have their own garden. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich find themselves called upon to create a Beatrix Potter-inspired one in an empty field on a budget of £2,500

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steve Fletcher experiences a blast from his past when he takes on restoring a vintage shop till, just like the ones he remembers from his childhood. Janine Savage from Dorset hopes wood aficionado Will Kirk can transform a very special dolls house custom made for her by her grandfather, while the Rev Richard Hayes arrives with a battered leather box containing a compact communion set, hoping its years of use can be made much less evident tghanks to the skills of Suzie Fletcher

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show, in which five pairs of contestants have to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle it out for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rylan Clark-Neal host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: Dennis recalls his youth in Coalisland and rise on the professional snooker circuit. While Dennis often reached tournament finals, he always seemed to falter - leading the press to class him as an eternal runner-up. In 1985, however, everything changed with Dennis going on to make the ultimate sporting comeback in a World Championship final watched by a record-breaking 18.5 million people people. He talks to Stephen Watson about his upbringing, the World Championship title win - and the origin of those famous upside-down glasses

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic rowing champion Katherine Grainger, former Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Michael Jamieson and decorated 400m runner Christine Ohuruogu join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Return of the comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby. Alison and Mike are trying to find a semi-harmonious routine with the ghostly housemates of Button House. The ghosts have found a way to make their new situation work for them, while the couple are working toward a new money-making venture to fund their perpetual renovations. But after a paranormal photo of the house goes viral, Alison and Mike spot an opportunity to bring money in more quickly by using the ghosts' 'talents' to create an authentic haunted-house experience

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is disappointed to discover she is not invited to Maria's hen party because of her age, and decides to gatecrash the event, only to end up at the wrong venue. She then tries to impress the bride-to-be's mother during a dinner date, but is so nervous she turns to drink, and the evening descends into chaos. Comedy, written by and starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Eddie, Helen, Tom and Elizabeth pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron apologises, Luke follows Victoria upstairs, and Cain offers Moira an olive branch, but it remains to be seen whether she will accept

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the lockdown, millions of people face joblessness, redundancy or uncertainty about their future on a scale unseen since the early 1980s. Robert Peston investigates the impact on the economy, with industries shutting down overnight, millions unable to work, and many more working from home for the foreseeable future. He also asks what can be done to avoid the mistakes of past recessions and assesses whether the Government is on the right track to avoid mass unemployment

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy fumes to realise Nate has been keeping a secret, a stranger named Meena arrives in the village, and Aaron has future hopes

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The financial expert is joined by Angellica Bell for another round of advice on family finances, jobs and consumer rights, including the latest deals of the week and tips on making money go further

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Rose West & Myra Hindley: Their Untold Story with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The veteran journalist tells the extraordinary untold story of how the two most notorious women in British crime, Rose West and Myra Hindley, became lesbian lovers in jail. Former Category A prisoners, including reformed Mafia member Marisa Merico and ex-gangster Linda Calvey, tell Sir Trevor how Rose West revealed her murderous temper in prison, while former prison governor Veronica Bird, police officers and prison visitors describe how Hindley cleverly manipulated people in jail, even persuading a female guard to help her escape

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Matthew Webb settles in and is introduced to the family but after a tour of his rubber plantation he is troubled to see that questionable practices are taking place. Joan sweeps him off to an amusement part with Monty and Ehrendorf, where Vera becomes intrigued by the newcomer, and soon he finds himself in the middle of a tug-of-war between Vera and Joan, battling for his affections. But very soon Singapore is hit by a war of a different kind. Adaptation of the novel by JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Georgia Blizzard and Elizabeth Tan

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the lockdown, millions of people face joblessness, redundancy or uncertainty about their future on a scale unseen since the early 1980s. Robert Peston investigates the impact on the economy, with industries shutting down overnight, millions unable to work, and many more working from home for the foreseeable future. programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is called to the University of Northumberland when Jamie Marshall, a promising young journalism student, falls to his death from the top of a disused faculty building. With no eyewitnesses and no immediate leads, Vera turns to friends and family to glean all she can about Jamie, and to discover who might have wanted him dead. The student's innocent facade is revealed to have concealed simmering resentment and anger which he expressed on the internet - and which may have made him some deadly enemies

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares dishes inspired by school dinners, including shepherd's pie with minted peas, mac and cheese with bacon and leeks, and jam and coconut sponge with custard. He is joined by guests Kimberly Wyatt, who whips up a cheese and bean tortilla, and Juliet Sear, who puts her own spin on a classic school dessert with her cornflake tart

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: The host and travelling companion Nick Nairn continue their Scottish adventure in Lossiemouth, home to the first RAF squadron and a fleet of typhoon fighter jets

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott enter the world of botany to search for the killer of a retired teacher found dead shortly after smuggling a rare orchid out of Borneo. The discovery of two more bodies makes the detectives realise the murderer will stop at nothing to get hold of the coveted plant, and it seems the key to the mystery may lie in a Latin notebook. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo takes over hosting duties on this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the lockdown, millions of people face joblessness, redundancy or uncertainty about their future on a scale unseen since the early 1980s. Robert Peston investigates the impact on the economy, with industries shutting down overnight, millions unable to work, and many more working from home for the foreseeable future. He also asks what can be done to avoid the mistakes of past recessions and assesses whether the Government is on the right track to avoid mass unemployment

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tracy fumes to realise Nate has been keeping a secret, a stranger named Meena arrives in the village, and Aaron has future hopes

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The financial expert is joined by Angellica Bell for another round of advice on family finances, jobs and consumer rights, including the latest deals of the week and tips on making money go further

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Rose West & Myra Hindley: Their Untold Story with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: The veteran journalist tells the extraordinary untold story of how the two most notorious women in British crime, Rose West and Myra Hindley, became lesbian lovers in jail. Former Category A prisoners, including reformed Mafia member Marisa Merico and ex-gangster Linda Calvey, tell Sir Trevor how Rose West revealed her murderous temper in prison, while former prison governor Veronica Bird, police officers and prison visitors describe how Hindley cleverly manipulated people in jail, even persuading a female guard to help her escape

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Matthew Webb settles in and is introduced to the family but after a tour of his rubber plantation he is troubled to see that questionable practices are taking place. Joan sweeps him off to an amusement part with Monty and Ehrendorf, where Vera becomes intrigued by the newcomer, and soon he finds himself in the middle of a tug-of-war between Vera and Joan, battling for his affections. But very soon Singapore is hit by a war of a different kind. Adaptation of the novel by JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Georgia Blizzard and Elizabeth Tan

programme.title: Celebrity Karaoke Club
programme.desc: Seven celebrities head into a karaoke bar with the hopes of winning this exciting music competition, performing everything from epic solo performances to group battles. Featuring contestants Scarlett Moffatt, Courtney Act, Jess Wright, Melvin Odoom, Tallia Storm, Luke Kempner and David Potts

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter's new friendship with a paramedic boosts his self-esteem, prompting him to dump his old pals, but he soon realises he cannot keep up with the man's active lifestyle

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire discovers the dating app Tinder, forcing Peter, Joe and Cleveland to intervene and save him from his soul-sucking hedonism. Meanwhile, Stewie discovers he has scoliosis

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A dark secret about the Smith family is revealed when Francine throws Stan a birthday party and invites his incarcerated father to the celebration

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Lindsey, the Smiths' regular babysitter (guest star Alison Brie), breaks her leg during a soccer match, and Hayley volunteers to look after Steve while Stan and Francine head out on a date. However, when the nerdy teen escapes his sister's attention, he and his friends embark on a quest to gain entry to a big house party, but end up crossing paths with an obsessive meth dealer

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the 'filming' of an episode - which is nearly ruined by a drunken Roberta, angry at having her storyline cut

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland loses his confidence and takes his family on a soul-searching journey to Africa, but gets sidetracked and spends a few days in Hawaii - where he has an epiphany

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, London Hughes, Nathan Caton and Not3s go up against Lady Leshurr, Darren Harriott, Ivo Graham and Yungen in the rap and hip-hop battle. Hosted by Jordan Stephens

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: A selection of some of the most incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts, including the most slam dunks by a parrot and the fastest time to put on and take off underwear. Matt Edmondson narrates

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des and Claire assure Becky they are not splitting up. Curly and Raquel get a shock when they spend an evening with the Malletts. Sue opens her heart to Ken

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des accuses Claire of looking for a reason to finish with him. Ken is horrified to find Sue unconscious in his hotel room. Don advises Ashley to be masterful with Kelly

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff visits Linda after her miscarriage. Sarah is furious with Rachel when she lets Andy go off with his dad. Zak wants to open his own museum at the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris makes a nasty remark to Biff about Linda's miscarriage. Mandy bans Butch from Charlie's party as Sophie will be there. Biff pours his heart out to Kathy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy and Walker hunt for a batch of stolen Russian icons, and suspect the victim of an assault at the Aidensfield Arms might be involved when they discover his criminal connections. Vernon embarks on another hare-brained scheme when he meets an Italian truffle salesman, while Helen is puzzled by a youngster's baffling medical history. Guest starring David Dickinson as antiques dealer Crispin Jones, with Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers why Jean is sulking and Sally and Mike will not talk to him. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: After resigning from his job as a traffic warden, henpecked George wonders how he can break the news to wife Mildred without incurring her wrath. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New broom Sgt Miller (John Duttine) introduces a rapid response car but his big city ideas receive a cool reception from the Aidensfield watch. Walker is beaten up by hoodlums but falls for nurse Clare Owen during his stay in hospital

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charlie demands to know what happened between Greg and Emma. Biff cannot face going home. The Dingles' medieval museum is nearly ready for opening

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony receives a mystery phone call. Sarah worries about Andy's behaviour since his dad returned. Kim catches Steve and Charlie in a romantic clinch

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kelly is upset that Ashley is trying to act macho. Sue apologises to Ken for all that happened in Southport. Claire and Becky move into the bookie's flat

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud reads Raquel's palm and tells her she is going to be travelling soon. The pensioner also urges Maureen to get passionate with Bill. Audrey and Alma go to a health farm

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The collapse of Owen Pierce's trial has left the case wide open and there's still a killer on the loose, but Banks remains determined to build a watertight legal case against his prime suspect. As the investigation continues, Morton and Jackman uncover promising new leads focusing on Ellie's ex-boyfriend Tyler, but just as they seem to be getting close to the truth, another schoolgirl is murdered, putting Pierce in the spotlight once again

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The housemates are put out when Chrissy's mother arrives for an unscheduled visit. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin gets the chance to play for his college football team - but a cold threatens to earn him an early bath. Flat-share comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Walker receives a tempting offer from an old friend, but as the proposition involves a spot of rule-bending on his part, the decision to go through with it could land him in serious trouble, especially as Sgt Miller already seems less than impressed by the policeman's rebellious nature. Rural drama starring, Jonathan Kerrigan, John Duttine and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sophie apologises to Zoe for the scene she made the night before. Charlie feels she has been used by Steve. Billy tells Jack and Sarah his intention to have Andy live with him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack urges Sarah to give Billy a chance to prove himself with Andy. Chris makes a speech at the Woolpack defending the prison plan. Mandy urges Lisa to be more forceful with Zak

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Claire is bemused about sleeping in a grotty flat. Ken acts as master of ceremonies at the school fete. Bill's plans to celebrate his birthday are spoiled

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken accuses Derek of spreading rumours about him and Sue. Des is upset when Claire calls him a conman. Audrey and Alma return from the health farm

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A village of Apern Tallow is rocked by a series of ghostly occurrences that begins with the inexplicable slashing of a painting in the local museum. Barnaby investigates, shadowed by Cully's actor boyfriend, who is preparing for a role as a police officer - but the mysterious events take a far darker turn when a man is found dead on the grave of his ancestor. Prunella Scales guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is worried about an intruder, and wonders whether to scare the man off or call the police

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid informs Jean he has been given a temporary promotion at work, but she soon begins to doubt what he says. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean worries about strange noises coming from next door. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in tow

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Community spirit in the village of Morton Fendle is dealt a blow by the collapse of a craft centre in which many locals had invested - and Alan Hollingsworth, the businessman behind the project, has become a public hate figure. Barnaby investigates when Hollingsworth's wife disappears, and seems to have been kidnapped. However, when Hollingsworth is subsequently murdered and his wife reappears safe and sound, he suspects an elaborate scheme. Roger Allam guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Frustrated that a number of cases have been knocked back by the Crown Prosecution Service, Banks and the team are called on to investigate the murder of teenager Ellie Clayton, daughter of a high-profile internet entrepreneur. The victim's background, together with the suggestion she may have been raped, means the incident is soon the subject of much media speculation, and strong evidence implicates a theatre director who ran a drama workshop the girl attended on the night she died. The detective believes he has found his man, but Morton fears her colleague isn't seeing the bigger picture

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is very surprised to find a well under his house. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. A man and woman claiming to be social workers visit a mother and tell her they need to take her son away. However, when they fail to bring him back, it transpires that the pair are not known by the authorities. Banks is drawn into a full-scale search for the missing boy, but new evidence and inconsistent witness statements cause the detective to question the truth of what he has been told. Co-starring Andrea Lowe, Caroline Catz, Christine Bottomley and John Lightbody

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is worried about an intruder, and wonders whether to scare the man off or call the police
